output.sar_narrative: Transaction 3519506 received a fraud score of 0.0379 per get_transaction_details, placing it well below the low-risk threshold of 0.40. get_shap_explanation shows that 5 of the top 6 SHAP contributors decrease fraud probability — notably V70 (SHAP -0.283), TransactionAmt at $35.95 (SHAP -0.258), and addr1=143 (SHAP -0.156) — with only tx_hour at 11.53 modestly increasing risk (SHAP +0.157). get_customer_history confirms the card (card1=4944) has been active during hours 11–13 in the prior 30 days, uses exclusively gmail.com and product type 'W', and that $35.95 is the minimum observed transaction amount, consistent with established spending behavior. The sole adverse action code returned by get_reason_codes is R07 (time-of-day anomaly), which appears weakly supported given the card's documented activity hours.

supporting_evidence.claim: The model's fraud probability score is very low at 0.0379, well below the low-risk threshold of 0.40.
The transaction amount of $35.95 is the lowest observed on this card over the past 30 days, indicating it is within normal spending range.
Five of six top SHAP features decrease fraud probability, with the strongest being V70 (SHAP -0.283) and TransactionAmt (SHAP -0.258).
The only risk-increasing SHAP feature is tx_hour (11.53), but card history shows the card is active during hours 11–13, reducing concern about this anomaly.
Card history confirms the card transacts exclusively in hours 11–18, consistent with the flagged transaction hour of 11.53.
The email domain gmail.com and product code 'W' in this transaction match the card's exclusive historical patterns.
get_reason_codes returned only a single adverse action code (R07: time-of-day anomaly), with no other fraud-positive signals.
supporting_evidence.value: adverse_action_codes: [{code: 'R07', description: 'Time-of-day or day-of-week anomaly for this account', driven_by_feature: 'tx_hour'}]
